lectures.title: The Word of Divine Purpose
lectures.headerQuote: "Your word is a lamp to my feet and a light to my path." Today we reflect on the transition of spirit into action.
lectures.firstReadingText: We set sail from Troas, making a straight run for Samothrace, and on the next day to Neapolis, and from there to Philippi, a district city of Macedonia and a Roman colony. We spent some days in that city.
lectures.firstReadingP1: On the sabbath we went outside the city gate along the river where we thought there would be a place of prayer. We sat down and spoke to the women who had gathered there.
lectures.firstReadingP2: One of them, a woman named Lydia, a dealer in purple cloth, from the city of Thyatira, a worshiper of God, listened, and the Lord opened her heart to pay attention to what Paul was saying.
lectures.reflectionText: The "opening of the heart" is often a quiet, riverside moment rather than a thunderclap. Lydia represents the receptive spirit that hears the divine call amidst the routine of life.
lectures.psalmResponse: The Lord takes delight in his people.
lectures.psalmVerse1:
Sing to the LORD a new song;
of praise in the assembly of the faithful.
lectures.psalmVerse2:
Let Israel be glad in their maker,
let the children of Zion rejoice in their king.
lectures.gospelText: Jesus said to his disciples: "When the Advocate comes whom I will send you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father, he will testify to me. And you also testify, because you have been with me from the beginning."
lectures.gospelP1: "I have told you this so that you may not fall away. They will expel you from the synagogues; in fact, the hour is coming when everyone who kills you will think he is offering worship to God."
lectures.gospelP2: "They will do this because they have not known either the Father or me. I have told you this so that when their hour comes you may remember that I told you."
lectures.commentaryP1: In this profound discourse, Jesus prepares his followers for the paradox of spiritual life: that divine truth often meets earthly opposition. The "Advocate" is not merely a comforter but a legal witness to the truth of the spirit in the face of judgment.
lectures.commentaryP2: The call is to remain steadfast in the knowledge of the Father's love, even when the structures of the world fail to recognize it. True worship is defined not by institutional compliance, but by the alignment of the heart with the Spirit of Truth.
lectures.meditationQuote: Do not let your hearts be troubled. You believe in God; believe also in me.
lectures.footerSubtitle: Curated Daily for the Faithful Soul
